content: The error in measuring the radius of a 5 cm circular rod was 0.2%. If the cross-sectional area of the rod was calculated using this measurement, then the resulting absolute percentage error in the computed area is __________. (round off to two decimal places)
explanation:
$$A = \pi {r^2}$$

For percentage error, $$\partial A = 2\pi r\partial r$$

$${{\partial A} \over A} = {{2\pi r\partial r} \over A}$$

$${{\partial A} \over A} = {{2\pi r\partial r} \over {\pi {r^2}}}$$

$${{\partial A} \over A} = 2\left[ {{{\partial r} \over r}} \right]$$ {$${{\partial r} \over r} = 0.2\% $$ given}

$$\therefore$$ $${{\partial A} \over A} = 2 \times 0.2 = 0.40\% $$
